name: American Airlines Federal Credit Union
description_short: American Airlines Federal Credit Union is a credit union offering checking, savings, auto loans, mortgages, and credit cards primarily to American Airlines employees and their families.
description_long:
American Airlines Federal Credit Union (AACFU) is a member-owned financial cooperative that has served the aviation industry for 90 years. The credit union specializes in providing financial products tailored to American Airlines employees, retirees, and eligible family members, leveraging its deep industry focus to build specialized lending and deposit products.

AACFU offers a comprehensive suite of financial services including deposit accounts (checking, savings, certificates of deposit), consumer lending (auto loans, RV/boat loans, home loans with multiple fixed and ARM options), and credit products (Visa credit cards for personal and business use). Their rates are competitive across categories: auto loans as low as 3.74% APR for new vehicles, home loans starting at 5.702% for 15-year fixed mortgages, and savings accounts offering up to 4.07% APY on ladder certificates. They provide online banking, ATM access via routing number 311992904, and business banking services.

What distinguishes AACFU is its airline industry specialization and member-focused approach as a credit union rather than a traditional bank. They currently advertise a 90th anniversary special offering up to 0.90% APR discount on auto loans. The organization emphasizes member education through their "Learning" section and maintains active fraud prevention messaging to protect member accounts from spoofing scams.

As a credit union, AACFU operates on a non-profit, member-owned model, which can translate to more favorable rates and lower fees compared to traditional banks. However, membership is restricted to American Airlines employees, retirees, and their families, limiting accessibility. The website provides current rate disclosures and compliance information but offers limited detail on membership eligibility verification processes or specific fee structures. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Competitive auto loan rates as low as 3.74% APR for new vehicles with 36-month terms
High-yield savings options with APY up to 4.07% on ladder certificates
Member-exclusive 90th anniversary promotion: up to 0.90% APR discount on auto loans
Mortgage options with multiple fixed and ARM products, 15-year fixed starting at 5.702% APR
Credit union membership model offering potential for lower fees and member-focused service
Business banking products including business checking and business Visa credit cards
Online banking platform with fraud prevention alerts and security messaging
cons: Membership is restricted to American Airlines employees, retirees, and eligible family members—not open to the general public
Limited transparency on website regarding specific membership eligibility requirements and verification processes
Credit card APR ranges are relatively high (12-18% for most products) compared to some competitor credit cards
Website lacks detailed information about fee schedules, account minimums, and overdraft policies without viewing full disclosures
No mention of mobile banking app features, mobile deposit, or other digital-first conveniences that competitors offer
best_for: American Airlines employees and retirees seeking employer-affiliated financial services
Airline industry employees looking for auto financing with industry-specific competitive rates
Families of American Airlines employees eligible for credit union membership benefits
Members seeking higher-yield savings products through CDs and certificate accounts
diagnosis: American Airlines Federal Credit Union is best for current and former American Airlines employees and their families seeking member-owned financial services with competitive rates on auto loans and mortgages. The primary caveat is strict membership eligibility limited to the airline industry, making it inaccessible to the general public; prospective members should verify eligibility before applying.
services: Checking accounts (Priority Checking and Flagship Checking)
Savings accounts and Share accounts with variable APY
Share certificates and ladder certificates with fixed terms
IRA accounts and retirement savings vehicles
New auto loans (3.74% APR for 36 months)
Used auto loans (4.24% APR for 36 months)
RV and recreational vehicle financing (6.99% APR)
Home mortgages (15-year, 30-year fixed and 5/1 ARM options)
Visa credit cards (Signature Cash Back, Platinum Rewards, Platinum Low Rate, Platinum Secured)
Business checking and business savings accounts
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
address: 6205 Waterford District Dr Suite 100, Miami, FL 33126
meta_description: American Airlines Federal Credit Union offers checking, savings, loans, and credit cards to AA employees and families.
